skill.name: notion-research-documentation
skill.description: Research Notion pages, synthesize findings, and create structured briefs, comparisons, or reports with citations.
skill.summary: Turn Notion research into cited documentation.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown inline-code spans as Ruby or shell execution, and those are false positives for tool names, file paths, and template references. The skill does include user-run Codex MCP setup commands and a hardcoded Notion MCP URL, which are legitimate for its Notion workflow but require user approval and OAuth setup.

medium_findings.title: User-Run Remote MCP Setup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add a remote Notion MCP server, enable the remote MCP client, and log in with OAuth. This is not automatic execution, but it changes Codex MCP configuration and connects to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible setup instructions and are directly related to the skill purpose. Risk remains because the workflow depends on a remote MCP endpoint and OAuth authentication.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most static external command findings are Markdown inline-code references to Notion tools, reference files, template folders, and example files. No evidence found that these lines execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code. The semantic context is task guidance for using Notion tools and reading reference documents.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at the description metadata lines. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection in those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines 3 and 5 only contain skill description metadata. There is no code path or instruction that uses a weak cryptographic algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Remote MCP Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains a hardcoded URL for the Notion MCP server. The endpoint appears relevant to the documented integration, but users should review it before adding it to Codex.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicit and tied to setup instructions for remote MCP access. It is not evidence of exfiltration, but it is a real network dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: CLI Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes Codex CLI commands for MCP setup and login. They are user-facing instructions, not bundled scripts, so command injection was not found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are static and documented in a setup section. There is no dynamic input, script file, or automatic execution mechanism in the scanned file.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time turning scattered Notion pages into useful reports. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to search, synthesize, cite, and publish structured Notion documentation.
actual_capabilities: Guides targeted searches across Notion pages before drafting.
Fetches relevant Notion pages through the Notion MCP integration.
Selects a brief, summary, comparison, or comprehensive report format.
Creates Notion pages with findings, citations, source links, and next steps.
Updates existing Notion pages with status notes or changelog entries.
limitations: Requires a connected Notion MCP server and user OAuth login.
Can only access Notion content available to the authenticated user.
Does not verify facts beyond the retrieved Notion sources.
Reference templates are mentioned, but only SKILL.md is present in this package.

use_cases.title: Prepare a research brief
use_cases.description: Summarize several Notion pages into a short brief with key findings and cited source links.

use_cases.title: Compare project options
use_cases.description: Collect Notion notes about competing options and turn them into a tradeoff comparison.

use_cases.title: Document internal knowledge
use_cases.description: Transform scattered workspace notes into a structured report for future reference.

prompt_templates.title: Create a quick brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short summary from known Notion sources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Notion for pages about [topic]. Create a concise brief with key findings, open questions, and links to each source.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a single topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a deeper research summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the most relevant Notion pages about [topic]. Synthesize the evidence into a research summary with themes, dated facts, and citations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare alternatives
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a decision support document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [option A], [option B], and [option C] in Notion. Create a comparison report with criteria, tradeoffs, risks, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Build an executive report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a comprehensive, publishable Notion page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Gather Notion sources for [initiative]. Create a comprehensive report with summary, evidence, risks, contradictions, recommendations, and a references section.

output_examples.input: Create a brief from Notion pages about the onboarding redesign.
output_examples.output: A Notion brief with a short summary, three key findings, cited source links, risks, and next steps.

output_examples.input: Compare the proposed analytics vendors using our Notion notes.
output_examples.output: A comparison page that lists evaluation criteria, vendor strengths, concerns, evidence links, and a recommendation.

output_examples.input: Turn all project discovery notes into an executive report.
output_examples.output: A structured report with context, synthesized findings, supporting evidence, open questions, and a references section.

best_practices: Confirm the research scope before fetching many Notion pages.
Track source page links while reading so citations stay accurate.
Flag missing, dated, or contradictory evidence in the final document.
anti_patterns: Do not publish a report from search results without fetching source pages.
Do not treat Notion notes as verified facts without attribution.
Do not run MCP setup commands without reviewing the endpoint and permissions.

faq.question: Does this skill require Notion access?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the Notion MCP integration and the user's authorized workspace access.

faq.question: Can it create pages in Notion?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes creating Notion pages when the Notion MCP tools are available.

faq.question: Does it include citation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the agent to track source IDs, links, and references while synthesizing.

faq.question: Can it compare options?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a comparison workflow for tradeoffs, risks, and recommendations.

faq.question: Does it run scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. The scanned package only contains Markdown instructions, including optional user-run setup commands.

faq.question: What should users review before setup?
faq.answer: Users should review the Notion MCP URL, OAuth permissions, and remote MCP client setting.
